dialogueChunks.intro: {player_name}, you don't know me, but I know when someone can be trusted. And right now I need someone I can trust more than anything.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: Our settlement signed a mutual defense pact with the garrison at {location} three seasons ago. We supplied them with food, they protected our borders. Simple, fair, sworn on oath. Then the {enemy_type} raids started and the garrison pulled back to protect their own walls. Left us exposed without a word of warning. {target_npc} rode to demand they honor the pact and was turned away at the gates. Now our stores are being raided nightly and we have no defenders. I need {quest_item} to fortify what's left and proof that the garrison broke their word.
dialogueChunks.outro: A sworn oath means nothing to them. But it means everything to us. Help me gather what we need to survive their betrayal.

playerResponses.text: Broken oaths deserve consequences. I'll help you build your case.
Gather supplies and evidence. I can do that.
Getting between a settlement and a garrison is too political for me.

dialogueChunks.intro: I should be angry. I am angry. But right now, {player_name}, what I really am is hurt.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: {target_npc} and I built this tavern together. Equal partners, equal shares, a covenant sealed with a handshake and witnessed by half the settlement. Last month, I discovered they've been secretly trading our best stock to {enemy_type} merchants {location_hint}: selling our finest barrels for personal profit and replacing them with watered-down swill. The regulars noticed the quality drop and started going elsewhere. Our reputation, everything we built together, is crumbling because of greed. I need {quest_item} to restock properly and evidence of the secret deals to bring before the settlement council.
dialogueChunks.outro: I trusted them with everything. My livelihood, my name, my future. They poured it all out for a few extra coins. I want justice, not revenge. There's a difference.

playerResponses.text: Justice means evidence. I'll find it.
Betrayal by a partner cuts deep. I'll help you set things right.
Business disputes aren't really my area.

dialogueChunks.intro: Come closer, {player_name}. This isn't a conversation for open air.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: The merchant guild in {location} signed a trade covenant five years ago: fair prices, shared routes, mutual support during shortages. It worked beautifully. Then the guild master started making side deals with outside traders, undercutting local merchants and funneling profits to a private account. {target_npc} uncovered the discrepancy in the ledgers and brought it to me. The guild master has powerful friends and {enemy_type} enforcers watching the roads. I need you to gather {quest_item} to support the local merchants who've been cut out, and retrieve the original covenant from the guild hall {location_hint}.
dialogueChunks.outro: Five years of trust, shattered by one person's greed. The covenant still exists: signed, sealed, legally binding. If we can put it in front of the right people, the guild master answers for every broken promise.

playerResponses.text: A binding covenant and a corrupt leader. I'll get you what you need.
Powerful friends and enforcers. This won't be easy, but I'm in.
Guild politics are a labyrinth I'd rather avoid.
